canon_fact_statement: >
  In canal-heartland and selected canal-adjacent heartland towns, "lock-night"
  is a named bardic-performance occasion distinct from ordinary tavern-night
  — performance around lock-closure hour, when lockmaster-crew, barge-layover
  travelers, and wharf-traders gather at the nearest tavern or lockmaster-
  hall; the occasion extends CF-0017 bardic-tavern economy with a canal-
  infrastructure-keyed performance subtype carrying characteristic audience
  composition (canal-labor heavy, not wholly tavern-regular) and customary
  silver compensation above a middling tavern-night rate.

# Canal Lock-Night: Named Bard-Performance Occasion at Lockside Canal-Town Taverns

## What the Artifact Implies

The travelogue's fourth paragraph contains the line: *"I played the lock-night at a town I can only call 'the one with the green shutters' and took silver enough to drink for a week. I drank it mostly in one."* The definite article "the lock-night" is doing the lifting here — Vespera deploys the term to a Brinewick literate reading audience without gloss, presuming the occasion-class is recognizable. The immediate context (lockmaster posting-walls, canal-lock infrastructure, silver-wage register per CF-0009) places the occasion squarely inside the canal-heartland bardic-circuit (CF-0017) but identifies a specific sub-occasion distinct from ordinary tavern-night.

The narrator's travelogue register is inflation-biased overall (per artifact frontmatter `desired_relation_to_truth`), but the lock-night reference is offhand rather than self-promotional — a professional register detail rather than a boast — which reduces the inflation-risk relative to the artifact's other canonizable claims.

## Why It Fits This World

Animalia's lived texture foregrounds canal infrastructure (CF-0015; "Canals carry the commerce" per WORLD_KERNEL Chronotope), canal-labor as ordinary livelihood (CF-0038; EVERYDAY_LIFE canal-heartland register), and bardic-circuit performance as a visible economic stream (CF-0017; CF-0038 canal-circuit taverns rotating bards by quarter-year). A named performance occasion keyed to lock-closure hour sits naturally at the intersection of these three institutional axes — canal-labor rest rhythm + tavern venue + itinerant-bard livelihood — and honors the WORLD_KERNEL "Season-bound labor" and "workdays, market days, feast days" chronotope without inflating the scale of any existing institution.

The tonal contract is preserved (lived-in, earthy, hazardous; "veterans are quietly proud and dryly mocking of glory-talk" — circuit-bards are the scene's chroniclers, working labor not heroic vocation). No cosmetic addition: the occasion changes labor (lockmaster-crew + canal-labor audience shapes scheduling and repertoire), economy (silver-differential pay register), and language (canal-register vocabulary entering bardic catalog).

## Narrator Reliability Assessment

Vespera Nightwhisper (CHAR-0001) is a working canal-circuit bard in her mid-twenties with direct CF-0017 / CF-0024 professional exposure (artifact frontmatter `author_profile` + dossier). The lock-night claim is firsthand — she played the occasion — and is peripheral to the travelogue's self-promotional spine, reducing inflation-risk. The artifact is the sole source for the specific "lock-night" NAMING, though the surrounding mechanics (canal infrastructure, bardic economy, lockmaster posting infrastructure, quarter-year circuit rotation) are partially committed by CF-0015 / CF-0017 / CF-0024 / CF-0038. Per the Phase 3 narrator-reliability mapping, firsthand + sole-source caps at `soft_canon` — promoted by the partial-support evidence-breadth pathway rather than demoted to `contested_canon`.

## Immediate Consequences

Within weeks-to-months of acceptance, downstream prose (EVERYDAY_LIFE.md; INSTITUTIONS.md §CF-0017 annotation; ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md canal-economy prose) would name lock-night as a recognized bardic-calendar occasion alongside ordinary tavern-night, with characteristic audience-composition (canal-labor heavy, barge-layover travelers, wharf-traders) and a customary silver-differential pay register. Lockside canal-town taverns would be understood to host lock-night performance occasionally or seasonally, depending on canal-traffic density. Circuit-bards would be recognized as distinguishing lock-night bookings from tavern-night bookings in their scheduling vocabulary.

## Longer-Term Consequences

Across years and into the next generation of bardic-circuit and canal-economy prose, lock-night would accrete its own sub-genre within CF-0017 — lockside-labor ballads, canal-route work-songs, barge-traveler goodbyes — and would feed back into the broader bardic catalog via circuit-bard repertoire circulation. Lockmaster / tavern-keeper informal coordination for lock-night booking would create a minor inter-institutional texture (the lockmaster's end-of-shift rhythm and the tavern's performance-calendar entangling in ways not present at ordinary tavern-night), adding a small propagation into settlement_life and institutional micro-workflow.

Canal-labor oral-register entering bardic-circuit repertoire via lock-night audience composition provides a channel by which labor idiom enters the broader bardic catalog, slightly thickening the EVERYDAY_LIFE canal-heartland register over time. Economy: canal-town bardic-economy becomes measurably lock-schedule-keyed rather than only tavern-regular-keyed; performer-income volatility would track canal-season opening/closing, lock-repair windows, and seasonal barge-traffic density, making lock-night bookings a small-but-recognizable canal-dependent livelihood sub-stream.

## Risks

- **Rule 4 globalization**: an adjudicator must ensure the occasion remains canal-corridor-scoped; non-canal heartland small towns without lock infrastructure do not have lock-night even as they host CF-0017 tavern-night. The scope-stabilizer (a) in `why_not_universal` carries this load.
- **CF-0038 Brinewick-exemplar absorption**: Brinewick's canal-circuit tavern-bard quarter-year rotation (CF-0038) is NOT lock-night; the two occasions coexist distinctly. Downstream prose must not collapse them into one.
- **Narrator inflation-risk**: the definite article "the lock-night" may reflect Vespera's literary register rather than pan-circuit institutional currency. Adjudication should seek corroboration from other canal-heartland bardic testimony before promotion beyond `soft_canon`; the partial-support pathway already flags this in the evidence-breadth audit.
- **No invariant stress**: AES-1 (heroism in coin and scars), AES-2 (ordinary-life register), CF-0024 labor-register — all honored without strain.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Moderate. `likely_required_downstream_updates` lists three files — EVERYDAY_LIFE.md (canal-heartland register extension), INSTITUTIONS.md (CF-0017 annotation), ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md (canal-economy annotation). No new CF subtree, no new institution; the card names a sub-occasion within an existing institutional frame. `integration_burden: 2` is accurate.

## Likely Story Yield

Modest-to-real. WORLD_KERNEL §Natural Story Engines lists "Bardic-circuit traveler who witnesses a ward breach and must decide what to sing" — lock-night is the natural performance venue where a canal-labor witness hears (and later the bard sings) such a song. Specific story engines activated: (i) the circuit-bard arriving at a lock-town just as the lockmaster-crew is between shifts, negotiating lock-night pay against a lockmaster's silver-weighing; (ii) a barge-layover passenger at lock-night hearing a canal-route ballad that names their destination town's missing kin; (iii) lock-night as the performance venue where the silenced-contractor (CF-0036) retires to listen and does not speak — canal-labor ballad-subgenre absorbing the silenced-contractor register naturally.

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon`. The partial-support evidence-breadth keeps soft_canon defensible against Rule 4 globalization; promotion to hard_canon would require corroboration from at least one additional canal-heartland bardic-testimony source. If canon-addition's adjudicator judges the occasion implicitly committed by CF-0038 (quarter-year tavern rotation at Brinewick exemplar density) and CF-0015 (canal infrastructure commercial spine) jointly, a `soft_canon` record-write would make the commitment explicit; if the adjudicator judges it already implicit, the card can be declined with a note that the occasion is derivable-canon under existing CFs.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 6a (Invariants)**: All 16 invariants tested; PASS across ONT-1/2/3, CAU-1/2/3, DIS-1/2/3, SOC-1/2/3/4, AES-1/2/3. The occasion does not touch ONT-1 sentience-embodiment (sentient cat-folk bard performing as ordinary labor); ONT-2 artifact-as-locus (no magic claim); CAU-1 costs (none claimed); CAU-3 ward-speech (none uttered); SOC-4 chartered-guild (performance is CF-0017 labor, not chartered artifact-handling); AES-1 heroism-as-labor register preserved.

**Phase 6b (Mystery Reserve Content Firewall)**: All 20 MR entries checked; every `overlap: false` recorded with per-entry rationale (see frontmatter). No narrowing or shaping of any MR entry. M-7 checked because Vespera's bardic-custody position is M-7-adjacent; the claim is about audience-composition / performance-occasion naming, not about specialist reputation-genealogy — no M-7 territorial trespass. M-19 checked because lock-night uses canal infrastructure; the claim makes no antiquity/cause assertion about the canal itself.

**Phase 6c (Distribution Discipline)**: `why_not_universal` populated with three stabilizers anchored to CF-0015 canal-infrastructure gating, CF-0017 parent institution, and CF-0038 named-polity-instance. CFs consulted: CF-0015, CF-0017, CF-0024, CF-0038.

**Phase 6d (Diegetic-to-World Laundering)**:
- **6d.1 Evidence-breadth**: `partial` — supporting CFs commit mechanics; artifact specifies the naming. Independent evidence: CF-0015, CF-0017, CF-0024, CF-0038. Proposed_status soft_canon defensible via partial-support pathway.
- **6d.2 Epistemic-horizon**: `pass` — Vespera is CF-0017 working circuit-bard; occasion is within professional horizon. Consulted PEOPLES / GEOGRAPHY / INSTITUTIONS per `author_position_consulted`.
- **6d.3 MR Positional**: `pass` — bardic-custody M-7 adjacency noted; claim content does not trespass M-7 specialist reputation-genealogy territory; M-19 canal-antiquity not touched. No positional flag.

**Phase 6f Repairs Applied**: None fired — all sub-checks passed on first pass.
